text: Fun fact: The floating Mario head in 1997's Mario Teaches Typing, voiced by Charles Martinet, is in fact the original Mario in Real Time (MIRT) technology used by Martinet himself to voice Mario, Wario and even Donkey Kong at trade shows since 1992 (though he first voiced the latter two in 1994 instead)—a whopping four years before Super Mario 64 was released as a launch title for the Nintendo 64! Additionally, the first game in which Charles Martinet voices Mario proper, as opposed to a floating, disembodied Mario head, is Mario's Game Gallery, released a year before SM64 came to the N64!
